sources.title: Pride and Prejudice by Jane Austen
sources.rightsBasis: Underlying work is public domain in the UK; Project Gutenberg plain-text edition; only short attributed excerpts are retained.

evidence.anchor: Netherfield Park is let at last?

evidence.locator: Chapter III; Darcy refuses to dance with Elizabeth
Chapter XIX; Elizabeth refuses Mr Collins
evidence.anchor: I am very sensible of the honour of your proposals, but it is impossible for me to do otherwise than decline them.

evidence.locator: Chapter XXXVI; Elizabeth reassesses herself after Darcy’s letter
evidence.anchor: Till this moment, I never knew myself.

evidence.locator: Chapter XLVI; Jane’s letter reports Lydia’s flight
evidence.anchor: My youngest sister has left all her friends--has eloped; has thrown herself into the power of--of Mr. Wickham.
her sentiments had undergone so material a change
There is a stubbornness about me that never can bear to be frightened at the will of others.
evidence.requiredAnswer: There is a stubbornness about me that never can bear to be frightened at the will of others.

evidence.anchor: My courage always rises with every attempt to intimidate me.
evidence.requiredAnswer: My courage always rises with every attempt to intimidate me.

evidence.anchor: Till this moment, I never knew myself.
evidence.requiredAnswer: Till this moment, I never knew myself.

evidence.locator: Chapter V; Mary distinguishes two ideas
evidence.anchor: Vanity and pride are different things, though the words are often used synonymously.
evidence.requiredAnswer: Vanity and pride are different things, though the words are often used synonymously.

evidence.locator: Chapter LVII; Mr Bennet jokes about society
evidence.anchor: For what do we live, but to make sport for our neighbours, and laugh at them in our turn?
evidence.requiredAnswer: For what do we live, but to make sport for our neighbours, and laugh at them in our turn?

evidence.anchor: You must allow me to tell you how ardently I admire and love you.
evidence.requiredAnswer: You must allow me to tell you how ardently I admire and love you.
